articles.title: Supreme Court strikes Colorado ban on conversion therapy
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that Colorado's law banning conversion therapy "regulates speech based on viewpoint."

articles.title: Domino, the warty frogfish, is the first of its kind to be raised in captivity
articles.summary: Scientists say the little fish may hold broader lessons for raising other marine species in captivity.

articles.title: Supreme Court Rejects Colorado Law Banning ‘Conversion Therapy’ for L.G.B.T.Q. Minors
articles.summary: The state and more than 20 others restrict therapists from trying to change the gender identity or sexual orientation of L.G.B.T.Q. clients under the age of 18.

articles.title: Former Alex Jones employee says: 'It was nonsense, it was lies'
articles.summary: Josh Owens spent four years as a video editor and field producer for Jones' Infowars media company. "It was all about making things look cinematic," he says. Owens' memoir is The Madness of Believing.

articles.title: Drivers Count Their Pennies as Gas Hits $4 a Gallon Because of War in Iran
articles.summary: Since the Iran conflict began on Feb. 28, gas prices across the United States have increased about 35 percent. They are now above $4 a gallon, and drivers are wincing.

articles.title: How the U.K.’s Leader Decides Which U.S. Bombers to Let Fly Against Iran
articles.summary: As the United States expands its armada of warplanes on British soil, Prime Minister Keir Starmer says he is trying to defend Britain’s interests while keeping the country out of war.

articles.title: Gas crosses $4 a gallon in the U.S. for the first time in 3 years
articles.summary: The war with Iran has driven up gas prices at a time when affordability is high on people's minds.

articles.title: U.S. Gas Prices Hit $4 a Gallon on Average, a ‘Headache’ for Drivers and Trump
articles.summary: A month since the first U.S.-Israeli attacks and Iran’s response effectively shut off Persian Gulf oil, drivers are paying significantly more to fill up.

articles.title: Trump Faces a Decision on Whether to Start a Ground War in Iran
articles.summary: The president wants a negotiation, but the Iranians say they are refusing until a cease-fire is declared. And while Marines and the 82nd Airborne Division offer new leverage, the risks escalate quickl

articles.title: 6 books named finalists for the 2026 International Booker Prize
articles.summary: The shortlisted titles include novels and novellas from authors and translators spanning four continents, with stories that range from Japanese-controlled 1930s Taiwan to the streets of Tehran in 1979

articles.title: Will $4 Gas Hurt Trump’s Approval Ratings? Here’s What History Shows.
articles.summary: Presidents since at least the Carter administration have seen their approval ratings tied to gas prices. But there are signs the correlation may be weakening.

articles.title: DHS resumes asylum decisions. And, Iran's strike injures over 12 U.S. personnel
articles.summary: The Department of Homeland Security has lifted its ban on reviewing asylum applications. And, NPR has confirmed that an Iranian strike injured over a dozen U.S. personnel.

articles.title: Iran war delays delivery of medical goods to other parts of the world
articles.summary: The war in Iran has slowed down international shipping, much of which contains medical and humanitarian goods destined for Asia and Africa.

articles.title: Trump tells Europe 'Go get your own oil,' Iran hits oil tanker off Dubai
articles.summary: Iran attacked and set on fire a massive Kuwaiti oil tanker off Dubai overnight, as Gulf states increasingly suffer the fallout from the war.

articles.title: Deaths in ICE Custody Are Growing, and Trump Defends Ballroom Plans
articles.summary: Plus, a three-minute, multimillion-dollar art heist.

articles.title: In a town close to the farmworker movement, some struggle to process Chavez allegations
articles.summary: March 31 is Cesar Chavez's birthday, and a longtime holiday. In the wake of sexual assault allegations against him, residents in the farming town of Delano are conflicted about how to remember him.

articles.title: Europe Has a ‘Guns vs. Butter’ Problem. War in Iran Makes It Worse.
articles.summary: After decades of prioritizing domestic over military spending, the continent’s leaders are trying to pivot. That is straining national budgets and could anger voters.

articles.title: Russia is building in parts of Ukraine. What does it mean for a peace deal?
articles.summary: Russia is heavily developing occupied territory in Ukraine, suggesting it has no plans to return the land as part of any peace deal. NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Reuters reporter Mari Saito.

articles.title: Figure skating season ends with redemption and heartbreak. What do fans watch next?
articles.summary: Worlds marks the last competition of the 2025-2026 season. Skaters have some time to go on tour, rest up and learn new routines before the next season starts in July.

articles.title: TSA workers may receive pay soon. And, Israel plans to expand its invasion of Lebanon
articles.summary: TSA workers have now been without pay for more than 40 days, as Trump says he has a plan to pay them. And, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announces plans to expand the invasion of Lebanon.

articles.title: Former national security adviser discusses the ongoing talks between the U.S. and Iran
articles.summary: NPR's Michel Martin asks Biden administration national security adviser Jake Sullivan for his reaction to the latest developments in the war in Iran.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
